description: Use `.context/` substrate blocks (decisions / framework-gaps / session-notes / conventions / rationale / tasks / work-orders / etc.) as the default persistence surface. Freeform MD writes (seed-round-plan.md DEC bodies, phases/discoveries.md DISC rows, ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.md events, ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.md preambles, commit messages) are a fallback for cases where the substrate path is genuinely gated (schema widen pending for that block; substrate block kind doesn't exist yet) or genuinely unsuitable (multi-file git commit messages, etc.). When MD writes ARE unavoidable: keep them tight — DEC bodies ~3-5 sentences; LOG events 1-3 sentences; commit messages summary + bullets only when needed (drop `What this does NOT do` sections, drop user-directive-quote chains, drop cross-reference enumeration); STATE preamble refreshes carry only the delta from the prior block. Cross-references + recovery come from git log + ORCHESTRATOR-LOG spine + prior STATE blocks; do not recreate that context inline on every write. Context window is operationally valuable; verbose MD entries burn it for diminishing returns when substrate types + relations.json edges express the same semantic content with schema-enforced concision.
